mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods with sustained payroll growth (> 100k monthly additions).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 3% signals aggressive monetary tightening that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of periods with rate hikes above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signal a disinflationary tightening environment that reduces earnings growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances with concurrent CPI decline and rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 115 signals robust manufacturing activity that supports corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of periods with INDPRO above 115.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real sales (RSAFS) above $330 billion signals strong consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods with RSAFS above $330B.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of periods with UNRATE below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) above 2,200k and GDP (GDPC1) above $11T signals a robust economic expansion that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances with both HOUST and GDPC1 above thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.5% combined with falling inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 2% signals a dovish tightening cycle that reduces bond yields and supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of periods with rate hikes and disinflation.
